new_classes.definition_en: The process of blending two or more single malt Scotch whiskies distilled at different distilleries to produce blended malt Scotch whisky.
Quality borne by a whisky that is a blend of two or more single malt Scotch whiskies distilled at different distilleries, all produced in Scotland.

new_classes.label_en: Blended malt whisky, in containers holding ≤ 2 l (CN 2208 30 41)
new_classes.label_de: Blended-Malt-Whisky, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 l (KN 2208 30 41)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended malt Scotch whisky produced by blending two or more single malt Scotch whiskies distilled at different distilleries, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the four key discriminating criteria for CN 2208 30 41: (1) the product is a Scotch whisky (producedBy a process occurring in Scotland); (2) it is a BLEND of two or more single malt Scotch whiskies distilled at DIFFERENT distilleries (BlendedMaltScotchWhiskyQuality + complement of SingleMaltWhisky); (3) it is made exclusively from malted barley (MaltedBarleyOnlyMashQuality, reusing existing quality); and (4) it is in containers ≤ 2 L (SmallContainer). The sibling code 2208 30 49 (blended malt whisky in containers > 2 L) is discriminated by the SmallContainer restriction. A minor gap: the legal text implies blending of whiskies from MULTIPLE (different) distilleries — this is captured qualitatively via BlendedMaltScotchWhiskyQuality but the exact cardinality constraint (≥ 2 distilleries) cannot be fully expressed as a simple OWL restriction without introducing a counter mechanism. Additionally, the sibling complement references SingleMaltWhisky22083030 as the most proximate sibling that needs explicit disjointness; further siblings (other whiskies under 2208 30) could be added when those classes exist in the TBox.
